name: San Francisco Mother
microbiome_notes: Lactobacillus sanfranciscensis dominant; first described 1971
notes: Iconic American sourdough; linked to Gold Rush era bakers c.1849

name: Ischia Starter
microbiome_notes: L. sanfranciscensis + Candida humilis; volcanic island microbiome
notes: Mediterranean island starter; low hydration stiff levain tradition

name: Finnish Ruis Juuri
microbiome_notes: L. brevis + L. plantarum; adapted to 100% rye fermentation
notes: Foundation of Finnish rye bread (ruisleipä); national staple

name: Swedish Surdeig
microbiome_notes: L. pontis + L. panis; Scandinavian rye adaptation
notes: Base for Swedish limpa and vörtbröd; malt-friendly culture

name: Danish Rugbrød Surdej
microbiome_notes: L. amylovorus + Pediococcus; cracked rye specialist
notes: Essential for Danish rugbrød (dense rye); 48-hour ferment tradition

name: Yukon Sourdough
microbiome_notes: Cold-adapted yeasts; survives sub-zero dormancy
notes: Gold Rush era culture; miners kept starters warm in sleeping bags

name: Icelandic Rúgbrauð
microbiome_notes: Thermophilic lactobacilli from hot spring baking tradition
notes: Baked underground in geothermal hot springs; unique microbiome selection

name: Egyptian Aish Baladi
microbiome_notes: Ancient lineage; L. plantarum complex
notes: Possibly oldest continuous culture; predynastic heritage

name: German Sauerteig Anstellgut
microbiome_notes: L. sanfranciscensis + L. pontis; strict German Reinheitsgebot-adjacent
notes: German rye bread backbone; Detmold three-stage process

name: Norwegian Surdeig
microbiome_notes: L. brevis + Leuconostoc; adapted to barley-rye blend
notes: Traditional Norwegian flatbrød and lefse culture; barley heritage

name: Russian Zakvasca
microbiome_notes: L. reuteri + L. pontis; cold-ferment adapted
notes: Base for Russian black bread (Borodinsky); cold cellar culture

name: Sardinian Madre
microbiome_notes: L. sanfranciscensis + S. exiguus; durum wheat specialist
notes: Pane Carasau and Carta di Musica tradition; very stiff

name: Alaskan Bush Starter
microbiome_notes: Cryotolerant Saccharomyces; survives -40°C dormancy
notes: Bush Alaska tradition; freeze-dried and revived seasonally

name: Swiss Bergbauer
microbiome_notes: L. plantarum + Weissella; high altitude adaptation
notes: Alpine farmhouse culture; maintained above 1500m elevation

name: Japanese Sake Kasu Tane
microbiome_notes: L. sakei + Pediococcus; sake lees crossover culture
notes: Fermented rice culture for rice bread; related to sake/miso tradition

name: Ethiopian Injera Ersho
microbiome_notes: L. pontis + Weissella confusa; teff-adapted microbiome
notes: Injera flatbread culture; extremely high hydration batter

name: Polish Zakwas
microbiome_notes: L. brevis + L. plantarum; Central European rye tradition
notes: Polish chleb żytni foundation; also used for żurek soup

name: Basque Artalde
microbiome_notes: L. sanfranciscensis; Atlantic climate microbiome
notes: Basque country bread tradition; maritime culture influence

name: Australian Outback Starter
microbiome_notes: Thermotolerant yeasts; survives extreme heat dormancy
notes: Outback station culture; dried on cloth and rehydrated

name: Tibetan Highland Tsampa
microbiome_notes: High-altitude adapted lactobacilli; low-oxygen tolerance
notes: Tibetan roasted barley ferment; monastery preservation

name: Mexican Masa Madre
microbiome_notes: L. plantarum + L. fermentum; corn-wheat co-culture
notes: Mesoamerican corn culture blended with Spanish wheat tradition

name: Scottish Barm
microbiome_notes: S. cerevisiae dominant; ale barm heritage
notes: Descended from brewery barm; oat grain adaptation

name: Latvian Rupjmaize
microbiome_notes: L. amylovorus + L. reuteri; Baltic rye culture
notes: Latvian dark rye bread culture; caraway-friendly ferment

name: Estonian Leivajuur
microbiome_notes: L. pontis + L. brevis; similar to Finnish ruis tradition
notes: Estonian rye bread foundation; related to Baltic sour traditions

name: Afghan Naan Khamiri
microbiome_notes: L. plantarum; high-altitude wheat culture
notes: Tandoor bread culture; dried for transport between villages

name: Georgian Puri
microbiome_notes: Ancient Caucasus lineage; wine yeast crossover
notes: Tone-oven bread culture; Fertile Crescent wheat heritage

name: Faroese Rúgbreyð
microbiome_notes: Sub-arctic adapted lactobacilli; maritime salt tolerance
notes: Faroese rye bread culture; survived extreme island isolation

name: Greenlandic Kaviak Bread
microbiome_notes: Psychrotolerant LAB; cold-dormancy specialist
notes: Arctic culture; naturally preserved by permafrost-adjacent storage

name: Svalbard Seed Vault Culture
microbiome_notes: Cryopreserved reference culture; research specimen
notes: Experimental culture maintained near Global Seed Vault; -18°C storage

name: Amish Friendship Starter
microbiome_notes: L. sanfranciscensis + robust S. cerevisiae
notes: Communal sharing culture; divided and gifted to neighbors

name: Tasmanian Heritage Wild
microbiome_notes: Wild-captured from orchard air; diverse Lactobacillus spp.
notes: Wild-captured starter; unique Southern Hemisphere microbiome

name: Korean Nuruk
microbiome_notes: Aspergillus + Rhizopus + LAB; parallel fermentation
notes: Korean fermentation brick; related to makgeolli and gochujang cultures

name: Sámi Barley Sour
microbiome_notes: Cold-adapted LAB; sub-arctic barley specialist
notes: Indigenous Sámi flatbread culture; barley-only tradition

name: Okinawan Sweet Potato
microbiome_notes: L. fermentum dominant; tuber-starch specialist
notes: Purple sweet potato ferment; subtropical island culture

name: Carl Griffith's 1847
microbiome_notes: Historic American culture; widely shared by mail since 1990s
notes: Oregon Trail heritage; Carl shared dried starter worldwide until his death in 2000
